body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: Anomaly Detection in the Open World: Normality Shift Detection, Explanation, and Adaptation

massive data so the the learning mechanism of deep learning based anomaly detection uh is called zero positive learning here positive means anomalies so zero positive learning uh means uh the Deep learning model are train with only normal data and typically there are two kinds of uh zero positive learning so the first one is reconstruction based uh learning and uh this is really achieved by uh uh generative models such as uh Gan uh Auto encoder VAR variational autoencoder so during training uh only normal data is fed into the model to minimize the rec construction Arrow between model input and model output after training it can uh detect noralist by uh Computing the Reconstruction error and uh the model is very likely to be failed when reconstructing anomalies which is a uh which are very different from the training normal data the second one is called uh prediction based models so uh uh which is usually used for time theories or sequential data and chied by uh such as lstm or recurrent neuron Network so during training uh given a normal time series with the L of T which is six in this example uh the sequential model like uh RNN will increase the probability of predicting the last simple at time t uh through the sequence with the last T minus one impulse so similar to reconstruction based uh models uh such model can predict the last simple in normal temp theories uh with higher probabilities but uh wrongly predict uh the anomalies uh time series uh okay this is the basic idea of uh deep learning based anomaly detection and so far researchers have developed deep learning based anomaly detection in waras uh network security applications such as Network inent detection log anomaly detection lateral movement detection and host based s detection unfortunately the great success of machine learning or deep learning are based on the close word assumption that is testing data must be similar to the trining data also known as the IID assumption however in open world uh settings the distributions of testing data can change over time in many unfor seen ways uh in machine Learning Community this problem is called concept drift problem here concept means uh the distribution of data and drift means the distribution uh has changed and uh concept drift can be quite common in security uh for example uh uh the change uh the statist the change of statistical features of uh mware during uh marware Evolution will make the uh performance of a malware classifier uh begin lower so uh uh Frankly Speaking the model AG in can be one of the most frustrating problem uh in real world applications built on machine learning or deep learning and we will see uh the great performance on the validation side but uh but overwhelming for positives or foral negatives in real deployment and this will easily make people lose the face of uh learning based methods so uh actually concept drift has been well studed in the context of uh supervised learning in security domain there are some of excellent work previously uh investigate concept drift in marware classification like I list here transcend and Cade and in machine Learning Community there are many uh related topics and one remarkable direction is uh the out of distribution detection so [Music] somewhere so uh as shown in the uh left picture if a sample doesn't belong to the distribution of existing classes it will be detected as a as an O sample however anomaly detection models are built on Purely uh normal data so they are immune to the sorry uh so they are immune to the drift of uh malicious or abnormal uh distribution changes but comes at the price of more severe impact when the distribution of normaly shifts here normaly means the distribution of uh normal data and normaly shift can also be quite common for uh for networking for security domains because uh the systems uh themselves and uh can differ and and involve over time such as some uh system patches or the involvement of new devices new users or new protocols and as shown in the in the right figure uh we uh uh if you only have the knowledge of normaly given a simple devat from the normaly we cannot tell whether it's a drift of normaly or it's a a real anomaly so so this can be a quite big challenge for detecting normal shift and previous uh previous work for superise learning is not suitable uh in this setting so that's why we highlight the difference of uh concept drift and normaly shift in the ttle of this slid so we summarize the uh first key inside is here for anomaly detection without gr Tru label anomality shift and real anomaly is not distinguishable and to mitigate concept drift or shift problem there are basically two pipelines uh the first one which is often used uh is to periodically retrain uh the model with uh new and old data or train a new one a new sub classifier to uh assemble however we think this is not suitable for practical applications uh because uh continuous training is labor intensive and hard to obtain uh the reason or the timing of uh shift so the second solution is to First determine and then investigate and finally uh adapt to the shift or the drift and this is our scope so the second uh inside here is we need to decide whether when and how shift occurs before adapting models to the shift uh to detect the shift we need to uh we need to uh to to figure out how to represent the distribution of data so ideally we want to uh capture capture the uh data from feature space to and detect the change uh however the high dimensional uh distribution is intractable in mathematics so uh an alternative way is to uh use the distribution of model output which is uh something like a probability ities or anomaly scores so in this way we are facing onedimensional score instead of high dimensional feature and uh so we summarized the uh key in set three is distribution of normaly can be represent by the distribution of model outputs uh okay with all all the three inside in mind we propose ow w a s for open word anomaly detection and which is a framework of detect and uh explain and adapt to noraly shift for deep learning based anomaly detection uh as you can see this is quite big and complicated figure and I will try to explain the motivation example uh in it uh so I suppose before any shift we have five symol in blue to represent the old distribution of normaly and we fed them into uh into the detection model to get the model outputs which are uh anomaly scores or probabilities and suppose after some shift we collect another file syles in Green from the new environment from the new distribution we also got their model output and um however as we see from the uh picture the origin model outputs are hard to uh distinguish between old and new uh distribution because they are all concentrated uh between 0.9 and one uh so the first step of O wad is to uh force a transformation on the original model outputs which we called Model calibration we expect the calibrated model outputs to provide probabilistic or statistical information uh of of of the sample so in this example we transfer uh file output file outputs in all distribution to be the percenti of uh original outputs which is uh one fifs two fifs so on and so fors so as we can see the distribution become more distinguishable after calibration uh however in practice there won't be only five samples so we need uh to compile uh the two distribution of calibrated outputs more strictly so that's why we introduced the Second Step shift detection and uh here we conduct a Hypes test on whether two sides are from the same distribution and use P value to determine whether there whether there is a shift so obviously in this example noraly has shift uh has shifted the next step is to investigate and explain the normaly shift uh here our goal is to find the most important symbols in the new distribution uh to uh that induced the shift we formulate an optimization problem to assign weight for each of the 10 samples from uh old and new distribution uh and the weight indicates whether the simple can represent the new distribution after shift uh like this uh the intuition is to use the mixture of old and new distribution uh to reconstruct the new normaly distribution as shown here uh and here we won't uh use all samples from the new distribution because new samples need to be labeled and we want to lower this uh labeling overhead so as shown from the feature space in this example we use three simples from uh the old distribution and two from because we won't let anomalies uh go into the uh to to the to the anomaly detection model finally in the last step we update the anomaly detection model for adapting to the shift we retrain the Deep learning model with uh the samples select from the uh last step as it can represent the new distribution and to uh award void forgetting useful knowledge when updating model we uh we we assign different weights for each sample which is exactly uh same to the weight uh derived from the formation shift explanation so uh this is the basic idea of O and note that uh this whole procedure can output calibration uh in in supervised learning model calibration is to transform classifiers scores into class membership probabilities uh for example if we have um one 100 predictions which are always uh model output of 0.8 after calibration then uh the ideal case is that exactly 80% of them should be correctly classified but for anomaly detection we don't have uh two class two class labels
